Title: The early stages of taxol biosynthesis: An interim report on the synthesis and identification of early pathway metabolites

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: T. brevifolia; T. cuspidata; T. canadensis (depending on enzyme/source discussed)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as an entry-point gene for heterologous taxane production; overexpress together with precursor-supply modules (MEP/IPP/GGPP) in microbial or plant chassis to boost taxadiene, but alone is insufficient because downstream oxygenations and acylations are required.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes formation of the first committed taxane skeleton precursor, taxa-4(5),11(12)-diene, enabling taxol biosynthesis and taxadiene overproduction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; heterologous expression in E. coli; kinetic comparison with native enzyme; X-ray structure; isotope-labeling/mechanistic studies with recombinant enzyme.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Temperature significantly affected taxadiene production in engineered E. coli; indole inhibited growth in heterologous production strains.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not reported in detail; isolated from yew saplings and cell cultures.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in Taxus saplings/cell cultures producing taxoids.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Global transcript profiling of E. coli production strains mentioned for host optimization; accession not reported.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Native enzyme is 862 aa preprotein with plastid targeting peptide; truncated recombinant enzyme 783 aa (~80 kDa) retains activity. Step is described as slow but apparently not rate limiting in native pathway. Taxadiene synthase also forms minor taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene as an adventitious product.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In heterologous hosts, production depends strongly on precursor supply and central metabolism; indole inhibits growth; enzyme step itself may not be native rate-limiting.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I diterpene cyclase/terpene synthase with plastid-targeting presequence
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid (predicted by transit peptide); recombinant truncated protein expressed solubly in E. coli
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Taxadiene synthase catalyzes the first committed step from GGPP to taxa-4(5),11(12)-diene. 2. Recombinant truncated enzyme expressed in E. coli mirrors native kinetics and enabled structural/mechanistic analysis. 3. It underpins metabolic engineering strategies that greatly increased taxadiene titers in heterologous hosts.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Taxus bark, heartwood, and cultured cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anticancer natural product used in chemotherapy

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Committed-entry enzyme for building microbial taxadiene platforms; should be stacked with upstream MEP/IPP/GGPP supply and downstream oxygenases/acyltransferases for full paclitaxel pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes (E,E,E)-geranylgeranyl diphosphate (GGPP) to taxa-4(5),11(12)-diene, with minor taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene byproduct formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases taxadiene formation, the first dedicated taxane intermediate toward taxol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Native enzyme purification; cDNA cloning; truncated recombinant expression in E. coli; enzyme kinetics; isotope-labeling; X-ray crystallography.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms first committed intermediate and taxane carbon skeleton for target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Temperature affects taxadiene production in engineered E. coli; indole inhibits host growth.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Obtained from yew saplings and cell cultures.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Host global transcript profiling in E. coli strains for production comparison; accession not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Remarkable terpene cyclase mechanism includes intramolecular proton transfer from C11 to C7 with high stereochemical fidelity. Step considered slow but apparently not rate limiting in native pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High production in heterologous systems requires extensive host optimization beyond this gene alone.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: First committed step; not established as rate limiting in native pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Taxadiene synthase generates the taxane hydrocarbon scaffold from GGPP. 2. Recombinant truncated enzyme enabled detailed mechanistic and structural studies. 3. Its expression in engineered microbes enabled gram-per-liter scale taxadiene production.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Yew; engineered E. coli; yeast; tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taxus spp.; Escherichia coli; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana tabacum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: T. brevifolia; Taxus sp.; not reported for hosts
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical for converting taxadiene to oxygenated taxane intermediates; useful in pathway reconstruction but may generate side-product OCT, so enzyme choice/context and redox partner design are important.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxa-4(5),11(12)-diene to taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol via oxidative allylic rearrangement; can also act on taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene and may rearrange substrate to OCT in some engineered contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the first oxygenated committed intermediate, taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol, but can also create off-pathway taxane byproducts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microsome incubations; radiolabeled substrate feeding; GC-MS and crystallization analyses; recombinant expression in Spodoptera microsomes and engineered microbes/plants; mechanistic inhibitor/cofactor tests.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate oxygenated precursor in early pathway
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydroxylation depends on NADPH and O2; inhibited by CO, miconazole, and clotrimazole in mechanistic assays.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not reported in provided text; CYP725A4 mentioned for rearrangement activity
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cytochrome P450 redox partners not specified
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence supports allylic radical mechanism rather than epoxide intermediate. CYP725A4 can mediate rearrangement to OCT without additional factors. Functional plasticity causes byproduct formation in engineered systems.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can form novel/off-pathway taxane OCT, diverting carbon from paclitaxel biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early oxygenation step; bottleneck status not firmly established here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Taxadiene 5α-hydroxylase converts taxadiene to taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol, the first hydroxylated taxane intermediate. 2. Mechanistic studies support allylic radical-mediated oxidative rearrangement. 3. In engineered hosts the enzyme can also generate OCT, revealing catalytic plasticity and possible flux loss.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to stabilize/channel early oxygenated taxane intermediates into downstream pathway steps; should be combined with 5α-hydroxylase and downstream hydroxylases, especially 10β-hydroxylase that prefers the acetate substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group to taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol to form taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-yl acetate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of acetate 27, an early downstream intermediate favored by some later hydroxylases.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synthetic substrate feeding/bioconversion studies; cloning and expression in E. coli; enzymatic characterization.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate downstream precursor for later oxygenation steps
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence indicates hydroxylation of taxadiene to 26 precedes acetylation to 27.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD-type acyltransferase (acyl-CoA dependent transferase; family not explicitly stated in text)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Taxadienol O-acetyltransferase acetylates taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol to acetate 27. 2. The enzyme was cloned from T. canadensis and functionally expressed in E. coli. 3. This acetylated intermediate is preferred by certain downstream hydroxylases, especially 10β-hydroxylase.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Introduces one of the predicted second/third hydroxylations; useful for pathway completion after 5α-hydroxylation and acetylation, but substrate preference must be respected.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene-5α-ol at C13α to form the corresponding 5α,13α-diol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of 13α-hydroxylated early taxane intermediates toward taxol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microsomal enzyme characterization; substrate feeding with labeled/unlabeled taxoids; gene cloning.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Originally obtained from T. cuspidata microsomes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to CYP725A family. Prefers taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene-5α-ol over acetate 27; did not further hydroxylate 10β/13α diols to triols under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No further conversion of some diols observed, indicating possible substrate restrictions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 13α-hydroxylase catalyzes an early hydroxylation of 5α-hydroxytaxadiene. 2. The enzyme prefers alcohol 26 as substrate. 3. It is a CYP725A-family P450 cloned from T. cuspidata.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important downstream oxygenase to pair with taxadienol acetyltransferase because it prefers the acetate substrate; can support ordered assembly of early pathway modules.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene-5α-yl acetate at C10β to form the corresponding 5α-acetoxy,10β-diol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces 10β-hydroxylated intermediates in early taxol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microsomal characterization; gene cloning; substrate preference assays with labeled/unlabeled compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Originally obtained from T. cuspidata microsomes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assigned to CYP725A1 gene line. Prefers acetate 27 as substrate. Did not further hydroxylate tested 10β/13α diols to triols.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate specificity may constrain pathway engineering if acetate substrate is limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 10β-hydroxylase catalyzes a predicted second/third oxygenation step in the pathway. 2. It prefers the 5α-acetate substrate 27. 3. The gene belongs to the CYP725A1 lineage and was cloned from T. cuspidata.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially needed for later pathway reconstruction, but acts on more oxygenated taxoids and not simple early taxadienes; should be introduced only after proper upstream tailoring steps are installed.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxusin-derived intermediates at C7β; acts sequentially before 2α-hydroxylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces 7β-hydroxylation on more advanced taxoids, supporting later taxol pathway elaboration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hydroxylation assays using (+)-taxusin and derived intermediates; gene cloning; catalytic and binding studies.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not reported beyond Taxus species origin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Very similar to CYP725A family. Does not accept less functionalized taxadienes or highly substituted taxoids efficiently, supporting an intermediate-stage role. Operates before 2α-hydroxylase.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Restricted substrate scope complicates heterologous pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 7β-hydroxylase acts on taxusin-like intermediates rather than simple taxadienes. 2. It functions before 2α-hydroxylation in sequence. 3. Substrate specificity indicates a later intermediate role in taxol biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A later tailoring oxygenase to deploy only with sufficiently advanced substrates present; sequential pairing with 7β-hydroxylase is important.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxusin-derived intermediates at C2α, typically after prior 7β-hydroxylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adds 2α-hydroxylation to more advanced taxoids in the taxol pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hydroxylation assays using (+)-taxusin derivatives; cloned gene studies; sequential bioconversion experiments.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Not reported beyond Taxus species origin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Similar to CYP725A family. Operates after 7β-hydroxylation and does not use structurally simpler taxoids effectively.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Narrow substrate scope may limit pathway engineering flexibility.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 2α-hydroxylase introduces a later hydroxylation on taxusin-derived substrates. 2. It acts sequentially after 7β-hydroxylase. 3. Its substrate preference supports a later role in the pathway rather than early taxadiene oxidation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Yeast screening system; yew source gene
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for completing early oxygenation network, but characterization remains insufficient; should be treated as provisional until product identity is confirmed.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively forms a 9α-hydroxylated early taxoid intermediate, though product confirmation is lacking.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vivo feeding of transformed yeast expressing candidate P450 cDNAs; tentative product detection only.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: cDNA clone identified tentatively; product not fully identified because of lack of material; enzyme not characterized.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Uncertain assignment may misdirect engineering efforts.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A candidate 9α-hydroxylase cDNA was identified by yeast-based screening. 2. The substrate used was taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene-5α-ol. 3. Product identity and enzyme function remain provisional.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Yeast screening system; yew source gene
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Taxol biosynthesis / off-pathway competitive oxygenation branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for suppression to reduce diversion of flux into abundant C14-hydroxylated shunt metabolites and improve paclitaxel yield.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates taxoid intermediates at C14β; converts taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene-5α-yl acetate,10β-diol to the corresponding 10β,14β-triol and can also generate 5α,14β-diol from 5α-ol adventitiously.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of C14-hydroxylated shunt metabolites rather than canonical taxol pathway products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vivo screening of transformed yeast carrying candidate P450 genes; recombinant enzyme characterization; substrate conversion assays.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch by diverting flux to off-pathway metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains typical P450 oxygen-binding and heme-binding motifs and conserved Cys444, with extra N-terminal residues. The paper explicitly suggests suppressing this gene to redirect pathway flux toward taxol.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activity diverts metabolic flux away from taxol into abundant 14-hydroxylated taxoids.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene-5α-yl acetate,10β,14β-triol; also 5α,14β-diol in aberrant metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Taxa-4(20),11(12)-diene-5α-yl acetate,10β-diol; can accept taxa-4(20),11(12)-dien-5α-ol adventitiously
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-switch/diversion enzyme rather than canonical control step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 14β-hydroxylase produces C14-hydroxylated taxoids that are likely off-pathway relative to taxol. 2. Recombinant enzyme was identified and characterized from candidate P450 screening. 3. Suppressing this enzyme is proposed as a strategy to redirect pathway flux toward taxol.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential late-pathway gene for side-chain installation; valuable for completion of paclitaxel biosynthesis in heterologous systems and should be combined with β-phenylalanoyl-CoA supply and downstream side-chain tailoring steps.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the β-phenylalanoyl side chain from β-phenylalanoyl-CoA to the 13-OH of baccatin III to form N-debenzoyl-2′-deoxytaxol.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Carries out the key C13 side-chain acylation step leading directly toward paclitaxel assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; recombinant enzyme assays; selective acylation assays with baccatin III and β-phenylalanoyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms advanced immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recombinant enzyme selectively catalyzes 13-O-acylation of baccatin III with β-phenylalanoyl-CoA to form N-debenzoyl-2′-deoxytaxol.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires supply of the unusual acyl donor β-phenylalanoyl-CoA.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Late decisive assembly step; rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Taxoid C13 O-phenylpropanoyltransferase installs the side chain at C13 of baccatin III. 2. The enzyme uses β-phenylalanoyl-CoA as acyl donor. 3. This reaction yields N-debenzoyl-2′-deoxytaxol, a direct advanced precursor toward taxol.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful late-pathway tailoring enzyme for advanced taxanes; may inform oxetane-ring stage engineering and advanced intermediate optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group to the tertiary C4 position of advanced oxetane-containing taxanes such as 4-deacetylbaccatin III.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports late acylation of advanced taxanes and provides evidence about the timing/chemistry of oxetane-ring related transformations.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant expression; regioselective acetylation assays on advanced taxane substrates.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of advanced precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study cited in review showed regioselective acetylation on C4 of advanced taxanes, informing alternative model for oxetane-ring biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBAT catalyzes regioselective acetyl transfer on advanced taxanes. 2. Its activity supports an alternative proposal for oxetane-related late biosynthetic events. 3. The enzyme functions in late paclitaxel precursor tailoring.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for supplying the unusual β-phenylalanine starter unit; should be co-engineered with C13 acyltransferase to enable late side-chain assembly in heterologous hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies β-phenylalanine precursor for C13 side-chain installation in taxol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical characterization; mechanistic studies; crystal structure referenced in review.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target-product side-chain assembly
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Precursor-supply step for late side-chain branch
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanism involves removal/interchange of pro-3S hydrogen and amino group, rebonding at C3 with retention of configuration.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires coordination with CoA activation and transfer steps for effective flux into paclitaxel.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key precursor-supply step; rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAM converts α-phenylalanine to β-phenylalanine. 2. This unusual amino acid is the side-chain precursor used for C13 acylation of baccatin III. 3. Structural and mechanistic studies support its stereospecific rearrangement chemistry.
